title: Fog of War — Visibility System
description: Create a FogOfWar class that tracks which tiles the player has seen. The player has a vision range that reveals nearby tiles. Previously explored tiles are shown dimmed, and unseen tiles are rendered black. The draw_fog method overlays fog on the canvas.
starter_code:
# GUI Dungeon Crawler — Step 10: Fog of War
# Visibility system with explored/visible/unseen tiles.

import tkinter as tk
import random
import shelve
import os


class GameWindow:
    """Main game window with canvas and status label."""

    def __init__(self, title='Dungeon Crawler', width=800, height=600):
        self.width = width
        self.height = height
        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.root.title(title)
        self.root.resizable(False, False)
        self.configure_grid()
        self.canvas = tk.Canvas(self.root, width=width - 200, height=height - 40, bg='black')
        self.canvas.grid(row=0, column=0, sticky='nsew')
        self.status_label = tk.Label(
            self.root, text='Welcome to the Dungeon!',
            fg='white', bg='#333333', anchor='w', padx=10
        )
        self.status_label.grid(row=1, column=0, columnspan=2, sticky='ew')

    def configure_grid(self):
        self.root.rowconfigure(0, weight=1)
        self.root.rowconfigure(1, weight=0)
        self.root.columnconfigure(0, weight=1)
        self.root.columnconfigure(1, weight=0)

    def set_status(self, text):
        self.status_label.config(text=text)

    def run(self):
        self.root.mainloop()


class TileMap:
    """Draws a tile-based map on a tkinter Canvas."""

    TILE_SIZE = 32
    tile_colors = {
        'floor': '#808080', 'wall': '#404040',
        'door': '#8B4513', 'treasure': '#FFD700', 'enemy': '#FF4444',
    }

    def draw_map(self, canvas, map_data):
        canvas.delete('all')
        for row_idx, row in enumerate(map_data):
            for col_idx, tile in enumerate(row):
                x = col_idx * self.TILE_SIZE
                y = row_idx * self.TILE_SIZE
                color = self.tile_colors.get(tile, 'black')
                canvas.create_rectangle(
                    x, y, x + self.TILE_SIZE, y + self.TILE_SIZE,
                    fill=color, outline=''
                )


class DungeonGenerator:
    """Generates a random dungeon as a 2D grid."""

    def generate(self, width, height, num_rooms=5, seed=None):
        if seed is not None:
            random.seed(seed)
        grid = [['wall'] * width for _ in range(height)]
        rooms = self.place_rooms(grid, width, height, num_rooms)
        self.connect_rooms(grid, rooms)
        self.place_items(grid)
        self.place_enemies(grid)
        return grid

    def place_rooms(self, grid, width, height, num_rooms):
        rooms = []
        for _ in range(num_rooms):
            rw, rh = random.randint(3, 8), random.randint(3, 8)
            rx = random.randint(1, width - rw - 1)
            ry = random.randint(1, height - rh - 1)
            for y in range(ry, ry + rh):
                for x in range(rx, rx + rw):
                    grid[y][x] = 'floor'
            rooms.append({'x': rx, 'y': ry, 'w': rw, 'h': rh})
        return rooms

    def connect_rooms(self, grid, rooms):
        for i in range(len(rooms) - 1):
            cx1 = rooms[i]['x'] + rooms[i]['w'] // 2
            cy1 = rooms[i]['y'] + rooms[i]['h'] // 2
            cx2 = rooms[i + 1]['x'] + rooms[i + 1]['w'] // 2
            cy2 = rooms[i + 1]['y'] + rooms[i + 1]['h'] // 2
            for x in range(min(cx1, cx2), max(cx1, cx2) + 1):
                grid[cy1][x] = 'floor'
            for y in range(min(cy1, cy2), max(cy1, cy2) + 1):
                grid[y][cx2] = 'floor'

    def place_items(self, grid):
        for row in grid:
            for i, tile in enumerate(row):
                if tile == 'floor' and random.random() < 0.05:
                    row[i] = 'treasure'

    def place_enemies(self, grid):
        for row in grid:
            for i, tile in enumerate(row):
                if tile == 'floor' and random.random() < 0.03:
                    row[i] = 'enemy'


class Entity:
    """Base class for all game entities."""

    def __init__(self, x, y, name, symbol, health=100):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y
        self.name = name
        self.symbol = symbol
        self.health = health


class Player(Entity):
    """The player character."""

    def __init__(self, x, y):
        super().__init__(x, y, 'Player', '@', 100)
        self.inventory = []
        self.gold = 0
        self.attack_power = 15

    def move(self, dx, dy, game_map):
        new_x = self.x + dx
        new_y = self.y + dy
        if 0 <= new_y < len(game_map) and 0 <= new_x < len(game_map[0]):
            if game_map[new_y][new_x] != 'wall':
                self.x = new_x
                self.y = new_y
                return True
        return False

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'x': self.x, 'y': self.y,
            'health': self.health, 'gold': self.gold,
            'inventory': list(self.inventory),
            'attack_power': self.attack_power,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data):
        p = cls(data['x'], data['y'])
        p.health = data['health']
        p.gold = data['gold']
        p.inventory = data['inventory']
        p.attack_power = data['attack_power']
        return p


def draw_entity(canvas, entity, tile_size=32):
    """Draw an entity on the canvas as a colored oval."""
    x = entity.x * tile_size + 4
    y = entity.y * tile_size + 4
    color = '#4488FF' if entity.symbol == '@' else '#FF4444'
    canvas.create_oval(x, y, x + tile_size - 8, y + tile_size - 8, fill=color)
    canvas.create_text(
        entity.x * tile_size + tile_size // 2,
        entity.y * tile_size + tile_size // 2,
        text=entity.symbol, fill='white', font=('Arial', 14, 'bold')
    )


class InventoryPanel(tk.Frame):
    """Side panel displaying player inventory and stats."""

    def __init__(self, parent, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(parent, **kwargs)
        self.stats_label = tk.Label(self, text='HP: -- | Gold: --',
                                    fg='white', bg='#222222', anchor='w', padx=5, pady=5)
        self.stats_label.pack(fill='x')
        tk.Label(self, text='Inventory:', fg='#AAAAAA', bg='#222222',
                 anchor='w', padx=5).pack(fill='x')
        self.items_listbox = tk.Listbox(self, bg='#333333', fg='white',
                                         selectbackground='#555555')
        self.items_listbox.pack(expand=True, fill='both', padx=5, pady=5)
        btn_frame = tk.Frame(self, bg='#222222')
        btn_frame.pack(fill='x', padx=5, pady=5)
        self.use_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text='Use', width=8)
        self.use_btn.pack(side='left', padx=2)
        self.drop_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text='Drop', width=8)
        self.drop_btn.pack(side='left', padx=2)

    def update_display(self, player):
        self.stats_label.config(text=f'HP: {player.health} | Gold: {player.gold}')
        self.items_listbox.delete(0, tk.END)
        for item in player.inventory:
            self.items_listbox.insert(tk.END, item)


class GameLoop:
    """Manages the game loop using tkinter after()."""

    def __init__(self, root, canvas, fps=10):
        self.root = root
        self.canvas = canvas
        self.fps = fps
        self.running = False
        self.game_state = {}
        self.keys_pressed = set()
        canvas.bind('<KeyPress>', self._on_key_press)
        canvas.bind('<KeyRelease>', self._on_key_release)
        canvas.focus_set()

    def start(self):
        self.running = True
        self.tick()

    def stop(self):
        self.running = False

    def tick(self):
        if not self.running:
            return
        self.update()
        self.render()
        self.root.after(1000 // self.fps, self.tick)

    def update(self):
        player = self.game_state.get('player')
        game_map = self.game_state.get('map')
        if player and game_map:
            if 'w' in self.keys_pressed or 'Up' in self.keys_pressed:
                player.move(0, -1, game_map)
            if 's' in self.keys_pressed or 'Down' in self.keys_pressed:
                player.move(0, 1, game_map)
            if 'a' in self.keys_pressed or 'Left' in self.keys_pressed:
                player.move(-1, 0, game_map)
            if 'd' in self.keys_pressed or 'Right' in self.keys_pressed:
                player.move(1, 0, game_map)
            self.keys_pressed.clear()

    def render(self):
        pass

    def _on_key_press(self, event):
        self.keys_pressed.add(event.keysym)

    def _on_key_release(self, event):
        self.keys_pressed.discard(event.keysym)


class Enemy(Entity):
    """An enemy entity with attack power and loot."""

    def __init__(self, x, y, name='Goblin', symbol='G', health=50,
                 attack_power=10, loot_table=None):
        super().__init__(x, y, name, symbol, health)
        self.attack_power = attack_power
        self.loot_table = loot_table if loot_table is not None else ['Gold Coin']


class CombatLog:
    """Stores and formats combat messages."""

    def __init__(self):
        self.messages = []

    def add(self, message):
        self.messages.append(message)

    def get_messages(self):
        return list(self.messages)

    def clear(self):
        self.messages.clear()


class CombatManager:
    """Manages turn-based combat between player and enemy."""

    def __init__(self):
        self.player = None
        self.enemy = None
        self.log = CombatLog()
        self.active = False

    def start_combat(self, player, enemy):
        self.player = player
        self.enemy = enemy
        self.active = True
        self.log.clear()
        self.log.add(f'Combat started: {player.name} vs {enemy.name}!')

    def player_attack(self):
        damage = max(0, self.player.attack_power + random.randint(-5, 5))
        self.enemy.health -= damage
        self.log.add(f'{self.player.name} hits {self.enemy.name} for {damage} damage!')
        if self.enemy.health <= 0:
            self.enemy.health = 0
            self.log.add(f'{self.enemy.name} is defeated!')
            self.active = False

    def enemy_attack(self):
        damage = max(0, self.enemy.attack_power + random.randint(-3, 3))
        self.player.health -= damage
        self.log.add(f'{self.enemy.name} hits {self.player.name} for {damage} damage!')
        if self.player.health <= 0:
            self.player.health = 0
            self.log.add(f'{self.player.name} has fallen!')
            self.active = False

    def is_combat_over(self):
        return not self.active or self.player.health <= 0 or self.enemy.health <= 0


class CombatDialog:
    """Toplevel window for the battle UI."""

    def __init__(self, parent):
        self.parent = parent
        self.dialog = None
        self.combat_manager = CombatManager()
        self.attack_btn = None
        self.flee_btn = None
        self.use_item_btn = None
        self.log_text = None
        self.player_health_label = None
        self.enemy_health_label = None

    def show_combat(self, player, enemy):
        self.dialog = tk.Toplevel(self.parent)
        self.dialog.title('Combat!')
        self.dialog.geometry('400x350')
        self.dialog.grab_set()
        health_frame = tk.Frame(self.dialog, bg='#222222')
        health_frame.pack(fill='x', padx=10, pady=5)
        self.player_health_label = tk.Label(
            health_frame, text=f'Player HP: {player.health}',
            fg='#4488FF', bg='#222222', font=('Arial', 12, 'bold')
        )
        self.player_health_label.pack(side='left', padx=10)
        self.enemy_health_label = tk.Label(
            health_frame, text=f'{enemy.name} HP: {enemy.health}',
            fg='#FF4444', bg='#222222', font=('Arial', 12, 'bold')
        )
        self.enemy_health_label.pack(side='right', padx=10)
        self.log_text = tk.Text(self.dialog, height=10, bg='#111111', fg='white',
                                 state='disabled', wrap='word')
        self.log_text.pack(fill='both', expand=True, padx=10, pady=5)
        btn_frame = tk.Frame(self.dialog, bg='#222222')
        btn_frame.pack(fill='x', padx=10, pady=5)
        self.attack_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text='Attack', width=10)
        self.attack_btn.pack(side='left', padx=5)
        self.flee_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text='Flee', width=10)
        self.flee_btn.pack(side='left', padx=5)
        self.use_item_btn = tk.Button(btn_frame, text='Use Item', width=10)
        self.use_item_btn.pack(side='left', padx=5)
        self.combat_manager.start_combat(player, enemy)
        self._update_log()

    def _update_log(self):
        self.log_text.config(state='normal')
        self.log_text.delete('1.0', tk.END)
        for msg in self.combat_manager.log.get_messages():
            self.log_text.insert(tk.END, msg + '\n')
        self.log_text.config(state='disabled')
        self.log_text.see(tk.END)

    def _update_health(self):
        if self.combat_manager.player:
            self.player_health_label.config(
                text=f'Player HP: {self.combat_manager.player.health}')
        if self.combat_manager.enemy:
            self.enemy_health_label.config(
                text=f'{self.combat_manager.enemy.name} HP: {self.combat_manager.enemy.health}')


class SaveManager:
    """Manages game saves using shelve."""

    def __init__(self, save_dir='saves'):
        self.save_dir = save_dir
        os.makedirs(save_dir, exist_ok=True)

    def save_game(self, slot_name, game_state):
        path = os.path.join(self.save_dir, slot_name)
        with shelve.open(path) as db:
            for key, val in game_state.items():
                db[key] = val

    def load_game(self, slot_name):
        path = os.path.join(self.save_dir, slot_name)
        try:
            with shelve.open(path, flag='r') as db:
                return dict(db)
        except Exception:
            return {}

    def list_saves(self):
        saves = set()
        if os.path.exists(self.save_dir):
            for fname in os.listdir(self.save_dir):
                name, ext = os.path.splitext(fname)
                if ext in ('.db', '.dat', '.dir', '.bak'):
                    saves.add(name)
        return sorted(saves)

    def delete_save(self, slot_name):
        path = os.path.join(self.save_dir, slot_name)
        for ext in ('', '.db', '.dat', '.dir', '.bak'):
            full = path + ext
            if os.path.exists(full):
                os.remove(full)


class FogOfWar:
    """Tracks tile visibility and exploration state.

    Tiles can be in three states:

    Attributes:
        vision_range: How far the player can see (in tiles).
        explored: Set of (x, y) tuples for tiles that have been seen.

# Test it
if __name__ == '__main__':
    gen = DungeonGenerator()
    dungeon = gen.generate(18, 16, seed=42)
    start_x, start_y = 5, 5
    for ry, row in enumerate(dungeon):
        for rx, tile in enumerate(row):
            if tile == 'floor':
                start_x, start_y = rx, ry
                break
        else:
            continue
        break
    player = Player(start_x, start_y)
    fog = FogOfWar(vision_range=4)
    fog.update_visibility(player.x, player.y)
    game = GameWindow(width=18 * 32 + 200, height=16 * 32 + 40)
    panel = InventoryPanel(game.root, bg='#222222', width=200)
    panel.grid(row=0, column=1, sticky='ns')
    tilemap = TileMap()

    def render_all():
        tilemap.draw_map(game.canvas, dungeon)
        fog.draw_fog(game.canvas, 18, 16)
        draw_entity(game.canvas, player)
        panel.update_display(player)
        game.set_status(f'Pos: ({player.x}, {player.y}) | Explored: {len(fog.explored)} tiles')

    loop = GameLoop(game.root, game.canvas, fps=10)
    loop.game_state = {'map': dungeon, 'player': player}
    original_update = loop.update
    def update_with_fog():
        original_update()
        fog.update_visibility(player.x, player.y)
    loop.update = update_with_fog
    loop.render = render_all
    loop.start()
    game.run()
test_code:
fog = FogOfWar(vision_range=3)
assert hasattr(fog, 'vision_range'), 'FogOfWar must have vision_range attribute'
assert fog.vision_range == 3, 'vision_range should be 3'
assert hasattr(fog, 'explored'), 'FogOfWar must have explored attribute'
assert isinstance(fog.explored, set), 'explored should be a set'
assert len(fog.explored) == 0, 'explored should start empty'
assert hasattr(fog, 'visible'), 'FogOfWar must have visible attribute'
assert isinstance(fog.visible, set), 'visible should be a set'
assert hasattr(fog, 'update_visibility'), 'FogOfWar must have update_visibility method'
assert hasattr(fog, 'is_visible'), 'FogOfWar must have is_visible method'
assert hasattr(fog, 'is_explored'), 'FogOfWar must have is_explored method'
assert fog.is_visible(5, 5) == False, 'Nothing should be visible before update'
assert fog.is_explored(5, 5) == False, 'Nothing should be explored before update'
fog.update_visibility(5, 5)
assert fog.is_visible(5, 5) == True, 'Player position should be visible'
assert fog.is_explored(5, 5) == True, 'Player position should be explored'
assert fog.is_visible(5, 4) == True, 'Adjacent tile should be visible'
assert fog.is_visible(50, 50) == False, 'Far away tile should not be visible'
fog.update_visibility(10, 10)
assert fog.is_visible(5, 5) == False, 'Old position should no longer be visible'
assert fog.is_explored(5, 5) == True, 'Old position should still be explored'
assert fog.is_visible(10, 10) == True, 'New position should be visible'
assert len(fog.explored) > 0, 'explored set should not be empty after updates'
acceptance_criteria: FogOfWar has vision_range, explored (set), and visible (set) attributes
update_visibility() clears visible set, then adds tiles within vision_range
All visible tiles are also added to the explored set
is_visible() returns True only for currently visible tiles
is_explored() returns True for any tile that has ever been visible
Previously visible tiles remain in explored after the player moves away
draw_fog() overlays black on unseen tiles and dark on explored-but-not-visible tiles
hints: Square visibility: for dy in range(-vr, vr+1): for dx in range(-vr, vr+1):
Add to sets: self.visible.add((player_x + dx, player_y + dy))
Fog overlay: use canvas.create_rectangle with fill='black' for unseen, fill='#000000' with stipple='gray50' for explored
